data.problem: A bakery is selling loaves of bread and pies. On the first day of sales, they sold 30 loaves of bread and 5 pies for a total of $510. They took in $690 for 30 loaves of bread and 20 pies on the second day. Find the price of each type of item.
data.solution_steps:
\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } b &\text{ = price of loaf of bread} \\ 
\text{Let } p &\text{ = price of pie} \\ 
 \\ 
30b + 5p &= 510 \\ 
30b + 20p &= 690 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
15p &= 180 \\ 
p &= 12 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
30b + 5(12) &= 510 \\ 
30b + 60 &= 510 \\ 
30b &= 450 \\ 
b &= 15
\end{aligned}

data.solution_steps:
\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } s &\text{ = price of student ticket} \\ 
\text{Let } g &\text{ = price of general admission ticket} \\ 
 \\ 
10s + 15g &= 465 \\ 
10s + 25g &= 675 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
10g &= 210 \\ 
g &= 21 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
10s + 15(21) &= 465 \\ 
10s + 315 &= 465 \\ 
10s &= 150 \\ 
s &= 15
\end{aligned}
